turns.speaker: user
turns.text: I'm really struggling with my teenager. They're always on their phone and seem to be ignoring me.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: That can definitely feel frustrating. Have you tried setting some ground rules about screen time? It might help create a clearer boundary.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: I've tried that, but it just seems like I'm not being heard. They get really defensive.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: It's understandable to feel frustrated when your efforts aren't working. Maybe try having an open conversation about why you're setting these rules and what you hope to achieve together.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: That sounds like a good idea, but I'm not sure how to approach the conversation without it turning into an argument.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Starting with 'I' statements can help keep the tone positive. For example, you could say something like, 'I feel concerned when we don't have much time together because I value our relationship.'
